Subject Registration

















Subject Registration                                

Plan and register for your subjects each semester. If you encounter any difficulties during the subject registration process, feel free to contact us.


















Preparation                                                                    
Check information related to subject registration.

Â 

Read more













Schedules                                                                    
Students will be invited to register for subjects each semester.
Â 

Read more

Step-by-step Instructions                                        

The following sessions provide step-by-step instructions for subject registration procedures:





[Session 1] Access eStudent

[Session 2] Preparation â Check subject registration records and start time




                                                        ViewÂ PDFÂ for details
                                                    





[Session 3] Preparation â Search subject and class timetable




                                                        ViewÂ PDFÂ for details
                                                    





[Session 4] Add Subject




ViewÂ PDFÂ for details







[Session 5] Drop Subject




ViewÂ PDFÂ for details







[Session 6] Change Class (Subject Group/Subject Component)




ViewÂ PDFÂ for details







[Session 7] Retake a Failed Subject




ViewÂ PDFÂ for details







[Session 8] Waitlisting for Subjects




ViewÂ PDFÂ for details







[Session 9] Indicate Interest in Service-Learning Subjects Requiring Selection (For students enrolled in Undergraduate Degree programmes)




                                                        ViewÂ PDFÂ for details
                                                    





[Session 10] Common Errors in Subject Registration

Cluster-Area Requirements











The intention of PolyUâs undergraduate curriculum is to provide a balanced and all-rounded education experience for you. CAR subjects are general education subjects that aim to nurture your intellectual capacity, global outlook, communication and critical thinking skills from a multidisciplinary perspective.Â Â 




There are four different cluster areas of CAR
CAR (A): Human Nature, Relations and Development
CAR (D): Science, Technology and Environment
CAR (M): Chinese History and Culture
CAR (N): Cultures, Organisations, Societies and Globalisation


There are two additional requirements in CAR:
i.Â Chinese Reading and Writing (CR/CW) Requirements*
ii.Â English Reading and Writing (ER/EW) Requirements
*Not applicable to non-Chinese speaking students or students whose Chinese standards are at junior secondary level or below




For the list of double-fulfilment of DSR/Major and CAR subjects, please click here.
Â 
*** If you wish to find out all the Cluster-Area Requirements subjects, please select "List All" inÂ "Academic Year & Semesters" below.
Â Please note that subjects requiring overseas travel are not available for registration during the Add/Drop period. 



You can search the subjects from below:







Cluster Area



-- Please select --
All
CAR (A)
CAR - English Language (For SY/AD students only)
CAR (D)
CAR (M)
CAR (N)

General University Requirements (GUR)













GUR for 4-Year Undergraduate Student 



 




Admitted in 2021/22 or before




Freshman Seminar


Language & Communication Requirements


Leadership & Intra-Personal Development


Cluster-Area Requirements


Service-Learning


Healthy Lifestyle






 




Admitted from 2022/23




Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics Requirement


Innovation and Entrepreneurship Requirement


Language & Communication Requirements


Leadership Education and Development


Cluster-Area Requirements


Service-Learning


Healthy Lifestyle






 










For the details of curriculum framework of the General University Requirements (GUR), please click here.

Academic Integrity













Academic integrity (å­ļčĄčŠ äŋĄ) refers to the honest and ethical manner in which academic work is done, whether it is an assignment, an examination, an oral presentation, or a research project or report.
PolyU views Plagiarism as a serious disciplinary offence.
It is a fundamental value that all students at PolyU are expected to uphold.
As part of the Universityâs continuous effort in maintaining a fair and honest learning environment at PolyU, an online tutorial has been developed to:

raise your awareness of the Universityâs expectations for honest academic behaviour
explain to you the importance of upholding academic integrity
provide you with information about what might constitute academically dishonest behaviour and plagiarism
explain ways for you to stay clear from academic misconduct and plagiarism



The following students are required toÂ complete the Online Tutorial on Academic Integrity on Blackboard:
    
4-Year Undergraduate students taking the subject ofÂ Leadership Education and Development. Students whoÂ have been granted exemption or credit transfer for the subject need to take the online tutorial separately; and
Senior Year Intakes and Articulation Degree Programme students taking the subject ofÂ Essential Components of General Education; and
Some Higher Diploma or Postgraduate students studying designated subjects or programmes as requested by Subject Leader or Programme Offering Department


The Online Tutorial is accessible on PolyU Canvas.
Upon completion of the Online Tutorial on Academic Integrity, you will receive a copy of eCertificate via PolyU Connect email account.

Â 
For more information, you can access:

Information of Academic Integrity at PolyU
Student Guide on Academic Integrity
Online Tutorial on Academic Integrity FAQsÂ 
Enquiry:Â academic.integrity@polyu.edu.hk

Â

Invitation to enrol in General Education Subjects offered by The Education University of Hong Kong and Lingnan University in Semester One, 2026/27
(For Local Current Students of Undergraduate Degree Programmes)


14 Jul, 2026



General
















2026/27 Semester One Subject Registration and Add/Drop Arrangements (including Adjustment before Semester Commencement Period)
[For Current Students of Taught Postgraduate Programmes (except Programmes 02018 & 05001)]


13 Jul, 2026



Subject Registration
















2026/27 Semester One Subject Registration and Add/Drop Arrangements (including Adjustment before Semester Commencement Period)
(For Current Students of Undergraduate Degree Programmes)


13 Jul, 2026



Subject Registration
















Release of Assessment Results for Summer Term of 2025/26 (Taught Programmes)
In connection with the release of assessment results for Summer Term of the 2025/26 academic year, we would like to draw your attention to the related arrangements.


6 Jul, 2026



Assessment Results
















Examination Timetable for Summer Term of 2025/26
The examinations for Summer Term of 2025/26 are scheduled to be held from 13 to 18 July 2026.


25 Jun, 2026



Examination Arrangements
















2025/26 Summer Term Application for Graduation (For Undergraduate students)
If you will be meeting the graduation requirements in Summer Term of the 2025/26 academic year, you should pay attention to the related arrangements and apply for graduation.


26 May, 2026



Assessment Results
















Release of Assessment Results for Semester Two of 2025/26 (Taught Programmes)
In connection with the release of assessment results for Semester Two of the 2025/26 academic year, we would like to draw your attention to the related arrangements.


13 Apr, 2026



Assessment Results
















Renewal of Student Identity Card (Semester Two of 2025/26)
If your student identity card is due to expire by the end of January 2026 and you will continue your study in Semester Two of 2025/26, please collect a new card from 26 January 2026 onwards.


9 Jan, 2026



General
















Must-read for success in your study


26 Sep, 2025



General
















Application for MTR Student Travel Scheme (Academic Year 2025/26)
If you are a full-time student of 25 years old or below, you are eligible to apply for the MTR Student Travel Scheme.  Please check your mode of study printed on your Student Identity Card.


13 Aug, 2025



MTR

Award Parchment












When your graduation status is confirmed and announced on eStudent, you will receive the following official certifications without filing any applications

Â 



Certification Type
Issuance Party
Issuance Timeframe
Remarks



Electronic Transcript of Studies



AR
Around a month after the announcement
To be retrieved on ACVP.hk




Paper Transcript of StudiesÂ 
(Complimentary Copy)

Faculty/School Office
Around a month after the announcement
To be mailed to the address 
            you registered to PolyU



Electronic Award Parchment



AR
Normally in the late afternoon on the concerned award date or the following working day

To be retrieved on ACVP.hk






Paper Award Parchment
Â 

AR

Around two weeks after the concerned award date


To be collected via courier or in person/by representative, where applicable




Â 
Academic awards are conferred thrice a year -
in March, July and OctoberÂ 
Â 



Graduation Status Confirmation
Award Date#
Availability of Electronic Version
Availability of Paper Version



On or before 10 March

15 March

Normally in the late afternoon 
on the Award Date or 
the following working day

Around early April



 From 16 March to 10 July

15 July
 Around earlyÂ August



From 16 July to 10 October

15 October

Around earlyÂ November






#The date to be printed on the award parchmentsÂ 












Arrangements on Award Parchment Collection                                    













For All Students
[except for those enrolled in Research Postgraduate (RPg) programmes or programmes delivered in Chinese Mainland]












éĒåæŊäļčŊäđĶįåŪæ (éįĻäšäļ­å―åå°æčŊūįčŊūįĻ)
For students enrolled in programmes delivered in Chinese Mainland












Â 












Notes to graduates:

The award parchments are retained for one year and, if uncollected, they may be destroyed without further notice.Â 
If graduates have unsettled matters with the University, e.g. outstanding debts to the University, or are subject to disciplinary action, the University reserves the right to withhold the issuance of (or retract) their award parchments or certifications of study.
The award parchment is an official document; no replica will be issued. Hence, please safekeep it.Â

32nd Congregation (May Session)








 











Academic Year 2025/26                                                

Congratulations on your successful completion of study in the 2025/26 academic year. The University conducted the 32nd (2026) Congregation (May Session), designated for PhD graduates only, at the Jockey Club Auditorium (JCA) on the PolyU campus on 2 May 2026.Â  Â  Please visit this webpage from time to time to get the latest updates.




























Video Playback









Direction to JCA




















Event Schedule Review

Key Action Dates









Remember to wear the right gown and hood

Academic Gowns

Stay connected with PolyU


As a PolyU graduate, you can enjoy ongoing support, exclusive benefits, and enriching opportunities from your alma mater. To get started, activate your Alumni eCard via the Alumni Portal, which not only grants you campus access but also unlocks a range of alumni privileges. We also encourage you to connect with local alumni associations, mainland networks, overseas associations, and alumni interest clubs to expand your network and stay engaged.
A wealth of resources enhancing your alumni experience is available on the PolyU Alumni website. If you have any questions, please feel free to contact the Alumni Affairs Office.Â

International WIE








 






International WIE Summer 2026
                                        

Are you looking for international exposure to broaden your career horizons? 
International WIE is here for you!
GEO's International WIE programmes equip you with knowledge of global practices and sharpen your ability to work professionally across different cultures. Since 2005, our WIE programmes have been paving ways for PolyU alumni to land competitive jobs at globally acclaimed enterprises.



























Previous participant of UK WIE Programme; currently serving at HSBC Private Banking









Previous participant of Spain WIE Programme; currently serving at Morgan Stanley









Past participant of Australia WIE programme; currently serving at BCG as a Senior Knowledge Analyst














Programme
Destination
Application Deadline
Information Session Time

Supplementary Documents

4:00 - 5:00 PM



Bloom of Youth
Supplementary Documents
Germany, Ireland, New Zealand, Spain, and UK
April 11, 2026 (July 2026 Programme)

January 19, 2026 (Monday)
4:00 - 5:00 PM

February 5, 2026 (Thursday)4:00 - 5:00 PM

3:00 - 4:00 PM

Finished




The Intern Group

Bangkok, Dublin, London, Madrid, and New York

*Students who are interested in the internship programmes offered by the providers listed above, please click HERE for application!




















Eligibility






Year 2 or above,Â full-time students of UGC-funded undergraduate degree programmesÂ 
Aged 18 or above with a valid travel document
Excellent English communication and writing skills










Selection, Support, and Others




Students applying for positions with Direct Employers are required to take selection tests arranged by GEO between November 2025 and February 2026. Only shortlisted candidates will be nominated to respective employers / partners starting from November 2025.

Chinese Mainland WIE








 






Chinese Mainland WIE Summer 2026
                                        

Join the Chinese Mainland WIE and dive into a world of diverse work cultures!Â  Experience the vibrant traditions and lifestyles of your placement city while gaining insider knowledge of the latest business and industry trends in the Chinese Mainland.












Join Chinese Mainland WIE Summer 2026!











Unlock your career potential with the External Programme organised by GEO! Gain invaluable workplace experience in the Chinese Mainland's major cities and boost your employability, giving you a competitive edge in the job market after graduation.

Looking for more opportunities? Explore for our [External Programmes] now!















Â Duration:
Â Any 6 - 8 weeks between May to August 2026Â 


Â Eligibility:
Â - Year 2 or above, full-time students of UGC-funded undergraduate degree programmes

            - Aged 18 or above with valid travel document(s)

            - Able to communicate in Putonghua in a work settingÂ 
Â 



Â Application link:
Â https://polyu.hk/uALgN


Â Application deadlines:

Â 1st Round: 27 February 2026 (Fri)
            Â 
Â 2nd Round: 27 March 2026 (Fri)
            Â 
Â 3rd Round: 17 April 2026 (Fri)

























Programme Details




To avoid any scheduling conflicts, please check your availability in advance and apply for theÂ Chinese MainlandÂ WIE Programme(s) that best align(s) with your schedule.

Application Process





Interview:Â A video interview will  be arranged to assess applicantsâ personal attributes and skill sets.  Applicants should also indicate their preferences on the location(s) and job  nature(s) in relation to discipline(s) during this interview.
Â 
Nomination:Â Based on the interview  performance, GEO may contact shortlisted applicants and ask about their preference  regarding whether to be nominated to employer(s) for internship position(s)  according to the indicated preferences and availability of vacancies.  Applicants can choose to accept or reject the nomination by replying to the related  email.
Â 
Offer:Â Upon the applicantsâ  acceptance of the nomination, GEO will follow up with the employer to confirm  the offer(s). Once the official confirmations are made by the respective  employers, offer notifications will be sent to the applicants, who are required  to decide whether to accept the offer(s) by submitting the Student Charter and completing other essential  information online.


Â

Semester Exchange








 




About Semester Exchange
All semester exchange places are offered to you via nomination by your Faculty/School/Department/GEO. The list of host institutions available to you differs for each academic year. Scroll down for options available for the current academic year.Â 








Â 
Am I Eligible to Apply?
You are eligible to apply if:

You are a full-time undergraduate degree-seeking student (local or non-local) in a UGC-funded programme.Â 
You have completed at least one year of study before the proposed student exchangeÂ 
    (For Senior Year Intake/ final year students, please check with your Faculty/School/Department for eligibility).
You fulfill the minimum requirement(s) on CGPA, English language, if any, set by your Faculty/School/Department/GEO and/or your preferred host institution(s).


Students who have previously enjoyed student exchange can also apply. Funding support might be limited on your second student exchange.Â 
Â 












How Do I Apply?                                        

 





1. Submit your Application




Select your preferred host institution(s) from the list of host institutions available to your Faculty/School/Department/GEO. Please note the application procedures and timelines may vary across these units. Carefully review and follow the specific process outlined by the relevant unit.
Choose your target host institution(s) based on your major/minor studies, subjects, credits required and budget. Make sure you fulfill the eligibility criteria and requirements set by host institutions, e.g. GPA, English proficiency and any restrictions). While CGPA of 2.75 is generally expected, some host institutions require higher GPA points. 
You may consider applying for the exchange programmes provided by your Faculty/School/Department or GEO.



1.1	Apply via your Faculty/School/Department
Below are the options available for the Academic Year 2025/26. Details on term dates, relevant links and any specific requirements set by the host institutions is provided. Research your preferred options to get familiar with them! Please note that all arrangements are subject to the latest announcements from the respective units.


Faculty of Business
Faculty of Computer and Mathematical Sciences
Faculty of Construction and Environment
Faculty of Engineering
Faculty of Health and Social Sciences
Faculty of Humanities

Faculty of Science
School of Design
School of Fashion and Textiles

School of Hotel and Tourism Management



Â 1.2 Apply via GEO
The GEO Student Exchange offers one-semester credit-bearing exchange opportunitiesÂ from host institutions around the globe. Applications should be made via GEO. Please refer to the GEO Student Exchange Guidelines for detailed information on application and the list of host institutions available for the GEO Student Exchange Programme.


Application Timeline:

1 Aprilâ 17 May 2026 (23:59 Hong Kong Time): Online application
May 2026: Interview (if applicable)
Mid-June 2026: Selected students to accept/decline the first-round application result (if applicable)
Mid-July 2026: Selected students to accept/decline the second-round application result (if applicable)








2. Get Nominated by your Faculty/School/Department/GEO




Successful candidates will receive an exchange offer notification from the Faculty/School/Department/GEO. Once you have accepted the offer, you will be nominated to your host institution by GEO. 
GEO must nominate you first to the host institution and when your nomination has been approved, the host institution will assess your application.






3. Apply to Host Institution




                                                        You will be nominated to your host institution by GEO. Upon receiving application materials from your host institution, you should make complete application by said deadlines (usually around three to six months before the start of your exchange semester). Consult your programme leader and/or departmental exchange officer on subject selection to ensure credits taken at your host institution are recognised and transferable back to PolyU. If your host institution requests your application documents to be sent via GEO, either by email or by post, you should consult GEO and send the full set of documents in required format at least two weeks, excluding public holidays, prior to the application deadline.

If your exchange application is successful, you will receive an enrolment/admission letter from your host institution. Be reminded to share a copy with us at outbound.exchange@polyu.edu.hk!
                                                    





4. Attend Pre-departure Briefing




                                                        After accepting the nomination, you need to attend the Pre-departure Briefing (November for coming Semester 2, May for coming Summer Term and next Semester 1) organised by GEO. Details on application to your host institution, pre-departure preparations, exchange funding, as well as post-exchange follow-ups will be provided.
                                                    





5. Get Familiar with Insurance Coverage




Students taking up official/endorsed activities outside of Hong Kong are covered by PolyU Group Travel Insurance, such as exchange programmes. The Group Travel Insurance covers 365/366 days for exchange, starting from the date you leave Hong Kong to the date you return. For insurance coverage and claim procedure, please click here.

Check carefully the insurance requirements of your host institution/country. PolyU Group Travel Insurance may not fulfil the insurance requirements set by your host institution/country/region. You may need to arrange additional coverage. Some require the purchase of designated health insurance for your exchange stay at that particular country/region/host institution.






6. Apply for Visa




                                                        Research well in advance if you need a visa for your exchange study. Understand the application procedure and timeline, and prepare supporting documents as per required.
                                                    





7. Arrange Accommodation




                                                        Understand accommodation options available at or recommended by your host institution. Apply as early as possible but do not pay for the accommodation until you receive the enrolment/admission letter. You are recommended to apply for on-campus accommodation whenever available, followed by private housing and/or trustworthy housing agent recommended by your host institution. It is NOT recommended to source options from web on your own without credibility.
                                                    





8. Apply for Exchange Funding




                                                        Details will be provided during the Pre-departure Briefing and in a follow-up email from GEO. Online application for exchange funding by said deadlines is required. No late application will be entertained!
                                                    





9. Prepare for Departure (Academic Matters)




                                                        Finalise your subject selection at host institution with your programme leader and/or departmental exchange officer and receive pre-approval for credit transfer (Form AR41a). Change your status at PolyU as âoff campusâ and release any pre-registered subjects at PolyU.
                                                    





10. Report Arrival and Keep in Touch




                                                        You are required to attend orientation and complete registration procedure at your host institution. Inform your PolyU department and family of your safe arrival. You are also suggested to register with the Consulate/Embassy in your host country.
                                                    





11. Complete your Exchange




                                                        A free copy of official transcript will be sent to GEO or directly to you. Request and pay for a copy from your host institution if they do not offer transcript for free.

Complete Form AR41c for your PolyU departmental endorsement on credit transfer as soon as possible. Successful credit transfer back to PolyU is a MUST, or the GEO exchange funding paid to you shall be clawed back.
                                                    





12. Share your Experience




As a PolyU exchange student, you are obliged to:

Be a PolyU Ambassador and help to promote exchange opportunities at PolyU to fellow students at your host institution.
Submit Post-Exchange report by said deadline.
Be a peer advisor at least twice after your return from exchange study to provide advice and sharing your experience to future outbound students at PolyU.

If a student does not fulfill the above obligations, PolyU reserves the right to claw back paid exchange funding.
















Credit Transfer Information
Successful credit transfer applications can be found in the Credit Transfer Database (for UG Programmes)Â here. It serves as a good reference point for your offshore learning planning. Please contact your programme offering departments directly to seek pre-approval and final endorsement upon the completion of your offshore learning activities.



Budgeting
You are paying PolyU tuition fee ONLY during your student exchange. Keep the following costs in mind when budgeting for an exchange:

Round trip ticket from Hong Kong/home country to host country
Accommodation
Visa application fees
Insurance
Daily expenses (food, laundry, etc.)
Textbooks and stationery

The costs of the above may vary from country to country, so make sure you check the host institutionsâ website for more information.


Below is an estimated summary of living expenses per month.

Financial Support                                        

Student exchange participations are supported by a range of financial assistance. Eligibility and exact amount vary across destinations and durations.
Your Faculty/School/Department may offer additional sponsorships and/or scholarships aim at covering expenses such as flights, accommodation or living costs. Check with your Faculty/School/Department and/or Student Affairs Office for further details.






GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange/GEO Funding for Student Exchange*




The Scholarship/Funding provides financial assistance to students participating in student exchange at PolyUâs student exchange partner institutions with tuition fee waivers and earn credit(s) upon completion of such exchange. The exchange period is one or two semesters, or at least four weeks in the case of clinical placement exchange.

You are eligible for the Scholarship/Funding:

This is the first GEO outbound exchange activityÂ involving physical travel during your entire undergraduate study; and
If you are a full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate student (local or non-local) participating in student exchange programmes; and
If you have attended all briefing sessions as required by GEO before departure.
In cases where candidates have equal merit, preference should be given to students with financial needs.Â 
When a student withdraws from the exchange programme after accepting the offer from Faculty/School/Department/GEO, the student may no longer be eligible for the GEO Scholarship/Funding for Student Exchange for any future student exchange activities.
GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange:


Full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate students have attained  a minimum cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 3.0 at the below mentioned time frame
The disciplinary record(s) of the student with PolyU will be taken into consideration


GEO Funding for Student Exchange:


Full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate students have attained below a cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 3.0 at the below mentioned time frame





Exchange Period


CGPA by Semester




Semester 1


Semester 2 of the previous Academic Year 




Semester 2

Summer Term


Semester 1 of the current Academic Year




Note: application details will be announced in the Pre-departure Briefing.
*Not applicable to students admitted in the 2025/26 academic year or later who are entry scholarship awardees.







GEO Exchange Grant




You are eligible to the funding:

Once onlyÂ during your entire undergraduate study;Â 
You are participating in student exchange for a period of at least one regular semester;Â 
You fulfil all the requirements for applying for GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange/GEO Funding for Student Exchange;Â 
You are a local student with a Hong Kong Identity Card; and
You are a recipient of grant under the Tertiary Student Finance Scheme â Publicly-funded Programmes by Student Finance Office.







GEO Funding for Student Exchange in Belt and Road Regions for Non-local UG Students




You are eligible to the funding:

Once only during your entire undergraduate study;Â 
You are a non-local student and enrolled in full-time undergraduate programme;
You fulfil all the requirements for applying for GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange/GEO Funding for Student Exchange; 
You are participating in student exchange programme in one of the "Belt and Road" regions except Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao; and
You have never received GEO Funding for Student Exchange in Belt and Road Regions for Non-local UG Students during the entire undergraduate study.Â Â 

Note: This funding is applicable to students participating in student exchange programmes from Academic Year 2025/26 onwards. 






Exchange Scholarship for Entry Scholarship Awardees




For students admitted in the 2025/26 academic year onwards, an additional HK$30,000 exchange scholarship is offered to all undergraduate students who receive the academic Entry Scholarship* to support their participation in student exchange activities.


Remarks: *In cases where candidates have equal merit, preference should be given to students with financial needs.


Â 






HKSAR Government Subsidy




Education Bureau of the HKSAR Government offers Means-tested and Non-means-tested Schemes in support of student exchange. Read below for eligibility:Â 
Means-tested Schemes

You are a local student with a Hong Kong Identity Card and enrolled in full-time undergraduate programme.


You are a recipient of grant under the Tertiary Student Finance Scheme â Publicly-funded Programmes by Student Finance Office (SFO); OR your family member is a recipient of the Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA).


i. Scheme for Subsidy on Exchange (SSE)

You participate in student exchange for four weeks or more in Semester 1 and / or 2.


You are eligible for SSE once only throughout your undergraduate studies.Â 
ii. Scheme for Means-tested Subsidy on Exchange to "Belt and Road" Regions (Means-tested SSEBR)

You participate in student exchange for four weeks or more in Semester 1 and / or 2.


You are eligible for Means-tested SSEBR twice at most at two different "Belt and Road" regions (except Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao) subject to maximum amount of subsidy to be received, i.e. HK$60,000, discounted by your level of student finance approved by SFO.

Â 
Non-means-tested Scheme
 i. Non-means-tested Subsidies on Exchange to "Belt and Road" Regions (Non-means-tested SSEBR)

You are a local student with a Hong Kong Identity Card and enrolled in full-time undergraduate programme.
You participate in student exchange in any "Belt and Road" regions except Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao.
You are eligible for Non-means-tested SSEBR once only throughout your undergraduate studies.Â 
 ii. Non-means-tested Subsidies Mainland Experience Scheme (Non-means-tested MES)

You are a local or non-local student and enrolled in full-time undergraduate programme.
You participate in student exchange in Chinese Mainland.Â 
You are eligible for Non-means-tested MESÂ once only throughout your undergraduate studies.

Special Remarks





You must declare to GEO if you receive sponsorship(s) and/or scholarship(s) from other source(s). You are required to provide relevant document(s) of such funding you receive.
PolyU reserves the right to withhold the payment of GEO Scholarship/Funding for Student Exchange/Exchange Grant/Government Subsidy if you do not declare other funding received (whether it is received before or after the approval of your application).

You are required to fulfill the obligations as per stated in the âHow do I apply?â section. If a student does not fulfill any of the obligations, PolyU reserves the right to claw back funding paid.

Incoming Exchange























Programme Information                                        




1. Incoming Exchange or Study Abroad




If your Home institution has a valid Student Exchange Agreement with PolyU, you can apply to PolyU as an Incoming Exchange student through nomination by your Home institution.Â 
Global Partners
If your Home institution does not hold an Exchange collaboration with PolyU, you are regard as a Study Abroad student.Â 






2. Study Load




                                                        Youâre required to enroll as full-time student during your Exchange/Study Abroad at PolyU. You are expected to complete a minimum of 9 credits and a maximum of 18 credits per semester. Most PolyU subjects carry 3 credits each (approximately 39 teaching hours), so we recommend taking 3-6 subjects per semester.
                                                    





3. Choose your Host Department




                                                        You will pick an academic department that offers the academic programme you are following at your Home Institution. Being attached to a Host department will give you priority in registering for subjects within that department. At least 50% of your enrolled subjects must come from the Host department. The rest of study load can be subjects offered by other departments. All registrations are subject to the approval of the respective departments.
                                                    





4. Fees




Tuition fee is waived forÂ Incoming ExchangeÂ students only. Refer to Fees and Expenses below for estimated budget for a semester of stay at PolyU.













See Exchange Leaflet and Exchange Factsheet

Nomination and Application Deadlines
Incoming Exchange nominations are to be made by your Home institution. Consult your Home institution for a valid Student Exchange Agreement with PolyU, on accurate information about exchange with PolyU, and its own requirements for exchange nomination. 

Study Abroad students please express your interest with us directly. Below deadlines apply. Click here for more information. 





Â 
Semester 1 (Sep â Dec)
Semester 2 (Jan â May)

15 Mar for School of Design
26 Mar for all other disciplines
15 Sep for School of Design
26 Sep for all other disciplines


Application:
15 Apr
15 Oct















How Do I Apply?                                        

 Follow the steps below to complete your Exchange/Study Abroad application. Click here for factsheet for details.Â 





1. Browse Courses and Subjects




                                                        Incoming Exchange/Study Abroad students can take subjects from all the departments/schools (check the requirements of each course) if the subjects are approved by your Home institution. Make sure at least 50% of your enrolled subjects come from your Host department.
                                                    





2. English Language Requirements




The medium of teaching at PolyU is English (except for language subjects). You should possess English proficiency equivalent to IELTS 6.0 or TOEFL 80 (internet-based). Incoming Exchange students are not required to submit proof of English proficiency at the time of application. However, you may be asked to provide such a document when needed. We recommend a GPA point of at least 2.7/4.0.Â  






3. Apply Online and Submit VISA Documents




Upon receiving nomination from your Home institution or your self-nomination in the case of Study Abroad, an invitation (a token) will be sent to you via email for making application. You are required to complete the online application with supporting documents uploaded, and dispatch or send by express post to Global Engagement Office (GEO) for our handling. A non-refundable administrative fee of HKD550 (covers VISA fee, courier service and courier of transcript etc.) applies.Â 
You need to pick a PolyU academic unit as your Host department and take at least 50% of Subjects from your Host. The Subject List available at the time you submit your application is for reference only. You will be able to read the finalized Subject List and timetables nearer to the start of the Semester.
Supporting document for upload:

Curriculum Vitae in English
Copy of identity document
    
Passport (with validity of 6 months after completion of exchange); or
Mainland ID Card and Mainland Residence Registration Card (åļļä―äššåĢįŧčĻåĄåŊæŽ); or


The information page of Taiwan Passport (with validity of 6 months after the completion of the exchange) and Taiwan ID Card and certified copy of Taiwan Census Paper (æķæŋäšåæčŠč­įæķįąčŽæŽ); or
Macao ID Card and Visit Permit for Residents of Macao SAR to HKSAR; or
Hong Kong ID Card


VISA application form:Â ID995A


Hong Kong Immigration Department
Guidebook for Entry for Study in Hong Kong (English VersionÂ /Â Chinese Version)


Recent identity photo
Copy of financial proof
Official transcript with grading scale (translation into English is required should your transcript is in another language)
List of subjects currently enrolled (if not recorded on Official transcript)
English proficiency proof of IELTS 6.0 or TOEFL 80 internet-based (if applicable)
Portfolio (for application to School of Design)

Specifications of supporting documents are available in the application instructions.
You are required toÂ dispatch or send by express postÂ the completed original pen-signed ID995A visa application form and required documents to GEO within 2 weeks after completed the online application.

















The Next Step                                        

Successful applicants will receive admission email about six weeks from application deadline. We will guide you on your next steps by email.
Enquiry should be sent to inbound.exchange@polyu.edu.hk by stating your name and Home institution.





1. Subject Registration




                                                        Final subject lists will be available in early August (semester 1) and early December (semester 2). Instructions will be sent by email to successful applicants in July and November respectively. Add/drop period is usually scheduled in week 1 and 2 of teaching weeks. It is important that you are flexible with your subject selection. Make sure at least 50% of your enrolled subjects must come from your Host department at PolyU.
                                                    





2. Accommodation




Incoming Exchange students enrolled into one of the undergraduate programmes at PolyU for semester-based study are guaranteed accommodation at the University-managed Accommodation (including the Student Halls and Off-campus Housing) for a twin-sharing room. No single room is available. The University reserves the right to assign the accommodation in the Student Halls of Residence or Off-campus Housing as and when necessary and appropriate. Application instructions will be sent to successful applicants in the admission email. Application period is usually in early August and early December. For Incoming Exchange student on clinical placement and non-semester-based study, PolyU accommodation offer is not guaranteed but we will try to accommodate as far as possible.
Study Abroad students are required to arrange their own accommodation in Hong Kong. We do advise students to find their accommodation before arriving in Hong Kong. Some housing information in the market can be found here.






3. Insurance




You will be responsible for your own medical and dental costs during your stay in Hong Kong. You are therefore required to take out an appropriate travel and health insurance scheme.
In addition to the University Health Services (UHS) provided by PolyU and the public medical services, PolyU has also arranged a Group Personal Accident Insurance for all PolyU registered students. The insurance only covers bodily injury occurs during activities organized by PolyU. Therefore all exchange students must enroll in an appropriate insurance plan to protect themselves in case of accidents and they will be requested to provide a copy of the Insurance certificate and policy before departure from home. The insurance plan should cover the entire exchange period with the start date and end date matching the studentâs flight departure date/return flight arrival date and the following items:

Medical expenses (including out-patient, hospital expenses and accident)
Personal accident (as the result of any illness, injury, disability or accident)
Personal liability covering the legal liability of causing the death of a third party or property damage due to negligence
Emergency medical evacuation and repatriation of remains (due to accident/illness/other causes).






4. Receiving VISA or Entry Permit




                                                        If your visa application is successful, we will send you instructions for downloading and printing the eVisa or courier the hard copy entry permit to your home or your Home institution. Â 

According to the condition of stay, Incoming Exchange/Study Abroad students are not allowed to take up any employment or volunteer work, whether paid or unpaid; or establish or join in any business. Please refer to The Hong Kong Immigration Department website for details.






5. Flight Arrangement




                                                        You can arrange your flight as soon as your travel dates are confirmed but it is advisable that payments should only be made after you have received your student visa from us. You should also pay attention to the terms and conditions of your tickets, particularly tickets at special fares.

You are expected to arrive approximately one week prior to the start of the semester and the exact date will be specified in your admission letter. Most exchange students depart after they finish the exam. The exam schedule will be announced in the middle of the semester. You are strongly advised NOT to confirm your return flight until you receive the exam timetable. However, if you have to confirm the return date at the time you purchase the ticket, please schedule your flight at the end of the exam period.






6. Important Dates









Â 
Semester 1
            (Sep - Dec)
Semester 2
            (Jan - May)


Hall residential period

25 Aug 2026 - 3 Jan 2027 


6 Jan 2027 - 28 May 2027

            (Attendance is compulsory)
26 - 28 Aug 2026 (tentative)
7 - 8 JanÂ 2027Â (tentative)

31 Aug 2026
11 Jan 2027


Class ends:
28 Nov 2026
17 Apr 2027


Exam period:
3 - 18 Dec 2026
22 Apr - 8 May 2027



For a full version of academic calendar:Â https://www.polyu.edu.hk/ar/docdrive/polyu-students/AC.pdf















Fees and Expenses
As an exchange student, you do not need to pay additional tuition fees to PolyU. You will, however, still be required to pay your tuition fees at your Home institution.
As a study abroad student, you will need to pay tuition fee to PolyU.
Whether you are an exchange student or study abroad student, make sure you keep the following costs in mind:

Return ticket
Accommodation
VISA application fees
Insurance
Daily expenses (food, laundry, etc.)
Text books and stationery

Â 
Below is an estimated summary of living expenses per month.



Items
HKD
USD


Accommodation (PolyU Student Halls of Residence)
2,100
270


Food
4,000
513


Laundry & air conditioning in residence
200
26


Miscellaneous expenses
900
115



Â 
Note:

One semester lasts for approximately 4.5 months.
The above estimates are for reference only. The actual amount required will vary depending on individuals' lifestyles and preferences.
The above does not include expenses for return flight tickets, local transportation, insurance, clothing, entertainment and textbooks.
Approximate exchange rate is USD1.00 = HKD7.80.

We will get back to you as soon as possible.

Summer Exchange












What is Summer Exchange?
The Summer Exchange Programme offers PolyU undergraduate students the opportunity to spend a summer at one of our partner institutions. All full-time PolyU undergraduate students in UGC-funded programmes, except final-year students, in any faculty/school are welcome to join the Summer Exchange Programme. Please click here to see a list of available destinations for Summer Exchange 2026.
Â 			
Am I Eligible to Apply?
You are eligible to apply if: 
- You are a full-time undergraduate degree-seeking student (local or non-local) in a UGC-funded programme (except final year students). 
- You have completed at least one year of study before the proposed student exchange
(For Senior Intake students, please check with your Faculty/School/Department for eligibility).
- You fulfil the minimum requirement(s) for CGPA, English language, if any, set by your Faculty/School/Department/GEO and/or your preferred host institution(s).











When and How to Apply?                                        




1. Complete Online Application Form




You are required to complete and submit the Summer Exchange Online Application Form by 22 January 2026, 23:59 (Hong Kong Time), providing the below information:Â 


Your latest CGPA for assessment of academic standing as required by host institutions (if applicable). 


Proof of English language proficiency, i.e. IELTS / TOEFL results. You may also submit DSE / IB / SAT / GCSE or other international qualifications for consideration. Please note that your host institution may require the IELTS / TOEFL results at a later date. 


Details of extra-curricular activities to demonstrate personal growth, leadership and involvement in community service. 


A personal statement (at a maximum of 150 words) explaining your reasons for application, including your personal attributes, your ability to represent PolyU, and your motivation for participating. 




 


Before making your selection, you are strongly encouraged to conduct thorough background research by reviewing the programme requirements or visiting their websites. The list of available destinations for Summer Exchange 2026 is available here.
You may select up to five preferred choices of exchange institutions/programmes. It is your responsibility to ensure that the chosen host institutions have the appropriate programmes and subjects to fulfil your degree requirements at PolyU. Please consult with the academic advisor/ exchange coordinator of your academic department regarding credit transfer. 
Late/incomplete applications will not be processed.


 








2. Application Review and Interview






The selection committee will review all applications according to the following criteria (but not limited to): 
Â 

CGPA
Extra-curricular activities
Personal statement
English language proficiency 
Interview performance (if applicable) 

Please note: If you have previously participated in a student exchange programme, you are still eligible to apply, but your application will be given lower priority.






3. Offer Announcement and Confirmation




GEO will announce application results to students via email in February.  Students should confirm their offer by the offer acceptance deadline.  Upon studentsâ confirmation, GEO will send out a confirmation email and nominate you to the host institution. Once you have received your confirmation of nomination, you can start preparing for the application to your host institution. You must submit your application to the host institution by the application deadline.  If the host institution requires you to submit your application via the home institution, you should consult GEO and send the full set of documents in the required formatÂ at least two weeksÂ (excluding public holidays) prior to the application deadline.  Late submission will jeopardise your submission. Final admission decisions will be at the discretion of the host institution.Â 














Credit Transfer 
Successful
credit transfer applications can be found in the Credit
Transfer Information (Outbound Activities) page here. It serves as a good
reference point for your offshore learning planning. Please contact your
programme offering departments directly to seek pre-approval and final
endorsement upon the completion of your outbound activities.


Â 
Budgeting
Keep the following costs in mind when budgeting for an exchange.


Round trip ticketÂ from Hong Kong/home country to host country
Accommodation
VISA application fees
Insurance
Daily expenses (food, laundry, etc.)
Textbooks and stationery

The costs of the above may vary from country to country, so be sure to check the host institution's website for more information.













Financial Support                                        

Students participating in the student exchange programme are supported by a range of financial assistance. Eligibility and exact amounts vary across destinations and durations.Â Details will be provided to successful participants during the Pre-departure Briefing and in a follow-up email from GEO. An online application for GEO funding by the specified deadlines is required. Late applications will not be entertained.Â 
Your Faculty/School/Department may offer additional sponsorships and/or scholarships aimed at covering expenses such as flights, accommodation or living costs. Check with your Faculty/School/Department and/or Student Affairs Office for further details relating to sponsorships/ scholarships.Â 





GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange/ GEO Funding for Student Exchange*




The Scholarship/Funding provides financial assistance to students participating in student exchange programme at PolyUâs student exchange partner institutions with tuition fee waivers and earn credit(s) upon completion of the exchange. The exchange period should be a minimum of 4 weeks for the Summer Exchange. 

You are eligible for the Scholarship/Funding:

This is the first GEO outbound exchange activity thatÂ involves physical travel during your entire undergraduate study; 
If you are a full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate student (local and non-local) participating in a student exchange programme; and
If you have attended all briefing sessions as required by GEO before departure.
In cases where candidates have equal merits on other selection criteria, preference would be given to students with financial needs.
When a student withdraws from the exchange programme after accepting the offer from Faculty/School/Department/GEO, the student may no longer be eligible for the GEO Scholarship/Funding for Student Exchange for any future student exchange activities.


GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange:



Full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate students who have attainedÂ a minimum cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 3.0Â in the below mentioned time frame.


The disciplinary record(s) of the student with PolyU will be taken into consideration.
    Â 




GEO Funding for Student Exchange:Â 



Full-time UGC-funded degree-seeking undergraduate students who have attainedÂ below a cumulative GPA (CGPA) of 3.0 in the below mentioned time frame.




Exchange Period
CGPA by Semester


Summer Term
Semester 1 of the current Academic Year



Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Â  Note: application details will be announced in the Pre-departure Briefing.
Â *Not applicable to students admitted in the 2025/26 academic year or later who are entry scholarship awardees.Â 

GEO Funding for Student Exchange in Belt and Road Regions for Non-local UG Students

You are eligibel for the funding:Â 

Once only during your entire undergraduate study;Â 
You are a non-local student and enrolled in a full-time undergraduate programme;Â 
You fulfil all the requirements for applying for the GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange/ GEO Funding for Student Exchange;Â 
You are participating in a student exchange programme in one of the "Belt and Road" regions except the Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao; andÂ 
You have never received GEO Funding for Student Exchange in Belt and Road Regions for Non-local UG Students during your entire undergraduate study.Â 



Exchange Scholarship for Entry Scholarship Awardees

For students admitted in the 2025/26 academic year onwards, an additional HK$30,000 exchange funding will be offered to all undergraduate students who receive the Academic Entry Scholarship to support their participation in student exchange activities.Â 

Â 






HKSAR Government Subsidy




The Education Bureau of the HKSAR Government offers Means-tested and Non-means-tested Schemes to support exchange activities. The eligibility is set out as follows:
Means-tested Schemes

You are a local student with a Hong Kong Identity Card and enrolled in a full-time undergraduate programme.
You are a recipient of grant under the Tertiary Student Finance Scheme â Publicly-funded Programmes by Student Finance Office (SFO); OR your family member is a recipient of the Comprehensive Social Security Assistance (CSSA).

Â i. Scheme for Subsidy on Exchange (SSE)

You participate in an exchange activity for at least 14 daysÂ (excluding travelling time).
You are eligible for SSEÂ once onlyÂ throughout your undergraduate study.

ii. Scheme for Means-tested Subsidy on Exchange to "Belt and Road" Regions (Means-tested SSEBR)

You participate in an exchange activity in any "Belt and Road" regions (except the Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao) for at least 5 daysÂ (excluding travelling time).
You are eligible for Means-tested SSEBR twice at most at two different "Belt and Road" regions, subject to a maximum amount of subsidy to be received, i.e. HKD60,000, discounted by your level of student finance approved by SFO.



Â Non-means-tested Schemes
 i. Scheme for Non-means-tested Subsidy on Exchange to "Belt and Road" Regions (Non-means-tested SSEBR)

You are a local student with a Hong Kong Identity Card and enrolled in a full-time undergraduate programme.
You participate in an exchange activity in any "Belt and Road" regions (except the Chinese Mainland, Taiwan and Macao) for at least 5 days (excluding travelling time).
You are eligible for Non-means-tested SSEBR once only throughout your undergraduate study.

Â  ii. Non-means-tested Mainland Experience Scheme (Non-means-tested MES)

You are a local or non-local student and enrolled in a full-time undergraduate programme.
You participate in an exchange activity in the Chinese Mainland. 
You are eligible for Non-means-tested MES once only throughout your undergraduate study.







Funding Amount (valid for 2025/26)





GEO Scholarship for Student Exchange / GEO Funding for Student Exchange for Summer semester (4 weeks or above AND less than 1 semester*)

(Attained a minimum CGPA of 3.0)

Special Remarks




You must declare to GEO if you receive sponsorship(s) and/or scholarship(s) from other source(s). You are required to provide relevant document(s) of such funding you receive.
PolyU reserves the right to withhold the payment of GEO Scholarship/Funding for Student Exchange/Government Subsidy if you do not declare other funding being received (whether it is received before or after the approval of your application).
As a PolyU Global Exchange student, you are obliged to:

Be a PolyU ambassador and help to promote exchange opportunities at PolyU to fellow students at your host institution;Â 
Submit post-exchange report within one month of the completion of your exchange period; and
Be a peer advisor at least twice and share your experience with future outbound students at PolyU after your return from the exchange study

If a student does not fulfil the above obligations, PolyU reserves the right to claw back any exchange funding provided.














In addition to the funding support managed by GEO, your Faculty/School/Department may also provide additional funding to support your exchange journey.Â The information below is subject to change without notice and is not exhaustive. Please contact your Faculty/School/Department directly for funding availability.
Â

Other Opportunities












Other Opportunities

HKSAR Government Subsidy
To encourage PolyU undergraduate students to participate in various short-term non-local activities available, the HKSAR Government is offering subsidies to eligible students undertaking eligible activities in 2025/26. Activities should be related to the discipline(s) of the degree being pursued.Â 
The following four schemes are exclusive to each other; students may only receive one of these subsidies at a time for each activity. Subsidy will be awarded to eligible students participating in eligible activities.Â 
Means-tested Schemes




Scheme for Subsidy on Exchange (âSSEâ);

Scheme for Means-tested Subsidy on Exchange to âBelt and Roadâ Regions (âMeans-tested SSEBRâ);Â 





Non-means-tested Schemes


 
Scheme for Non-means-tested Subsidy on Exchange to âBelt and Roadâ Regions (âNon-means-tested SSEBRâ); 
Non-means-tested Mainland Experience Scheme (âNon-means-tested MES).

Â

Eligible Activities





Fee-paying short-term programmes listed on Global Classroom and Exploring China or activities endorsed by academic units.
Programme-related study tours or field trips arranged by academic units. Endorsement from the academic unit is required.

International competition or conferenceÂ that is reputable/influential in the field and is beneficial to students in terms of personal, academic and professional development. Endorsement from the academic unit is required.

    Remarks: This Subsidy supports physical non-local learning activities only and is only applicable to the eligible activities listed above.Â  For students participating in Student Exchange Programmes and Summer@OxBridge, applications for the HKSAR Government Schemes should be submitted through the designated funding application provided by GEO.Â

Subsidy Amount


Means-tested Schemes
The Subsidy amount is calculated by applying the approved level of student finance to the maximum level of subsidy based on the latest available application result from SFO. The financial assistance level to be adopted in the calculation of the amount of financial level will be adjusted upward to 50% if the level of student finance assistance approved by SFO is lower than 50%.Â 







Formula = (Destination + Duration) x Grant % (in HK$)

Application Details







Activity Happens in


Semester 1 and Winter Break
(1 September 2025 to 11 January 2026)


Semester 2 and Summer Term
(12 January to 30 August 2026)




Application Period


1 November - 30 November 2025


15 May â 15 June 2026




Application Instruction




Applications for activities taking place in Semester 1 and the Winter Break are now closed. 




Â 
Individual Application
Students submit a complete application to GEO with proof of confirmed participation in the offshore learning activities.
Online application formÂ (for activities that take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term)
Â 
Group Application
For a group of students participating in the same programme/activity, the relevant Academic Units (Faculty/School/Department) should submit a complete application to GEO.
The online group application form (for activities that take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term) will be sent separately to academic units. 




Result Announcement


15 January 2026


31 July 2026




GEO informs the assessment result to individual/academic units.




Report Submission


15 February 2026


15 September 2026
            (activities take place in Semester 2 and Summer Term) 




Individual Application
Students submit full activity reports and official transcripts/certificates of completion in electronic copy to GEO. A report submission link will be provided to successful applicants.
Â 
Group Application
Academic units submit activity reports to GEO for each group of students. The report template will be provided to successful applicants.




Fund Disbursement


by 31 March 2026


by 31 October 2026
            (activities take place in 
            Semester 2 and Summer Term) 





Â 
Guidelines and Rules


Only programme fees, registration fees, travel expenses and accommodation costs are eligible for the Subsidy.
Should the total cost of the activity fall below the approved amount, GEO reserves the right to adjust the pre-approved financial support as necessary.
Applicants must meet all eligibility criteria prior to approval for the Subsidy.Â 
If any student withdraws from the approved activity at any time (before or during the activity), their eligibility for the Subsidy will be forfeited.Â 
Subsidy is limited and may be distributed on a first-come, first-served basis.
The final decision is subject to the review of the application and the necessary documents submitted by the specific deadline.

								                    Minor
							                    


Minor


















Â 

The Major plus Minor study option allows undergraduate students to explore an additional field of study alongside their Major. With over 30 Minor programmes available, students have exceptional flexibility to tailor their academic experience while maintaining a manageable study load. Admission to a Minor normally requires a GPA of 2.5 or above, making it an accessible and rewarding way for students to broaden their knowledge and skills.
Â 
Explore our Minor programmes:
List of Minors for Undergraduate Degree Programmes
Â 











Key Information                                        




Who can take a Major plus a Minor?




Undergraduate students are eligible to pursue a Major plus a Minor, except for the following cases:

Students admitted to the advanced stage of a programme on senior year status;
Students admitted to an articulation degree programme; or
Students enrolled in Majors which are designed to preclude the possibility of pursuing a Minor.

Students must obtain a Cumulative GPA of 2.5 or above at the time of the Minor application. Minor programme offering Departments may set quotas and impose additional criteria apart from the above GPA requirement.Â 
Â 






What are the credit requirements for a Minor?




A Minor programme is a collection of subjects totalling 18 credits with at least 50% (9 credits) of the subjects at Level 3 or above. Students opting for a Minor may be allowed to count up to 6 credits from their Major (excluding âFree Electiveâ subjects, if any) or General Universty Requirements (GUR) towards their chosen Minor.
Â 






What is the study duration for completing a Minor?




Students are expected to complete their Minor study during the normal duration of their Major study (or within the Maximum Period of Registration for students admitted in or before 2019/2020).






How to apply for a Minor?




Students interested in pursuing a Minor should submit Form AR147 to their programme offering Department during the application period. Applications will be considered by both the Major and Minor programme offering Departments.
Please refer to the schedule below:

Application period: 2 to 8 July 2026
Result notification period: 9 to 24 July 2026


Stay tuned for relevant University announcements nearer the time.
Â 




Â 

















More about Minor
                                        
If you have questions about your studies, our FAQ database is a great place to start. We have answers to many of the most common questions students ask.

Secondary Major














 





The Secondary Major option offers undergraduate students the opportunity to expand their expertise by pursuing a Secondary Major alongside their Major. This pathway provides a meaningful expansion of studentsâ academic portfolios, striking a balance between challenge and feasibility. It allows students to develop complementary skills and enhance their career prospects through a substantial yet manageable commitment.
Â 
Currently, the Secondary Major is available in two specialised areas:


Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA) - [Â curriculum structureÂ ]


 Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE) - [Â curriculum structureÂ ]


Â 
ExploreÂ our Secondary Major programmes:
List of Secondary Majors for Undergraduate Degree Programmes
Â 











What is  a Secondary Major?






What is  a Secondary Major?
An expanded area of studies
on top of an existing major; forming an additional award option:


Major + Secondary Major (AIDA or IE)








Who can opt?






Who can opt?
Cumulative GPA âĨ 2.70



Cumulative GPA âĨÂ 2.70 by the end of Semester Two of Year One








When to apply?






Please apply
before
the commencement of the second year of study


Applications open from 16 to 22 June 2026 and the application results will be announced before 29 June 2026.
















How to graduate?






To graduate, you must complete the Secondary Major
as part of
your graduation requirements.


You will receive one award parchment, which will list the title of the Major and the Secondary Major.

















Key Information                                        




What is a Secondary Major?





A Secondary Major features an expanded area of studies in addition to an existing major, forming an additional award option ofÂ "A Major plus a Secondary Major". The University currently offers two Secondary Major options for students: "Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA)" and "Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE)".
Check out different Major/Secondary Major options among Schemes/Programmes (PDF)Â 
Application Options for Different Departmental Scheme/Individual Programme (2025/26 Cohort)







What is the curriculum structure of "A Major + a Secondary Major"?





The curriculum structure of a "A Major + a Secondary Major" [i.e. Major + Artificial Intelligence and Data Analytics (AIDA) or Major + Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IE)] is as follows:




Major Requirements
[including Capstone Project and Discipline-Specific Language Requirements]

At least 54 credits


Secondary Major Requirements
36 credits*


General University Requirements 
27 credits


Free Electives Requirements
At Least 6 creditsÂ 


Minimum credit requirements for graduation
At least 123 credits



* At least 50% of the subjects should be at Level 3 or above.
Students are expected to complete the âMajor + Secondary Majorâ within the normal duration of the major programme.
Students may count up to 12 credits of their Major/GUR subjects towards the Secondary Major. Nevertheless, students must take at least 12 credits from their chosen Secondary Major in order to satisfy the residential requirement of the chosen Secondary Major.
If deemed appropriate by the programme offering Department, students are allowed to take a Major with a Secondary Major and a Minor. Subjects already double-counted for the Major and Secondary Major cannot be used to fulfil the Minor requirement.Â 
Â 








Who can opt for a Secondary Major?





Studying a Secondary Major is a free choice by students and not mandatory. Only students with a Cumulative GPA of 2.70 or above may be considered for Secondary Major enrolment. Each Secondary Major may stipulate additional selection criteria for admission.
Normally, the option to study for a Secondary Major will not be applicable to students admitted to the advanced stage of a programme, nor to students admitted to an articulation degree programme. On the other hand, students admitted on the basis of advanced standing may be allowed to study for a Secondary Major, if so decided by the programme offering Department. This decision will be made at the time of admission, based on the merits of each individual case.
Â 






How to apply for a Secondary Major?





Students must apply viaÂ eStudentÂ (Application Forms > (AR164) Application for Major/Secondary Major/Double Major) and obtain approval from the programme offering Department, during the designated application period. Please stay tuned for relevant University notice nearer the time.






How to graduate with a Secondary Major?





Students must complete the Secondary Major as part of their graduation requirements. For details, please refer to the curriculum structure of "A Major + a Secondary Major" and the curriculum structure of AIDA/IE above.
Students who wish to withdraw from the Secondary Major must obtain approval from the programme offering Department normally before the end of the add/drop period of the last semester of study. Students who were approved to pursue a Secondary Major in AIDA or IE but later failed to meet all the requirements may be eligible to graduate with a Minor in AIDA or IE as an exit award, provided they complete the necessary graduation requirements.
Students graduating with a Secondary Major will receive one award parchment, which will list the title of the Major and the Secondary Major.
Â 




























Why do we offer AIDA and IE as Secondary Majors?

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. 													Scholarship Types
												


Scholarship Types












PolyU recognises the academic and/or non-academic achievements of our students through offering different types of scholarships to our students. Normally, these scholarships are awarded to current students pursuing full-time undergraduate programmes leading to PolyU awards (i.e. HKCC, SPEED, PolyU inbound exchange students are not eligible).
Â 












Entry Scholarships for Prospective Students - awarded on the basis of admission results




PolyU offers entry scholarships to students pursuing different programmes during admission. Please refer to the table below for further information.





Programme pursued


Where to get scholarship information?

Enquiries



Full-time undergraduate programmes


Entry scholarships are available for outstanding admittees based on academic or non-academic achievements.Â  Please visit Undergraduate AdmissionsÂ webpage for details and further enquiries on scholarships offered to local,Â internationalÂ studentsÂ as well asÂ åå°åšåąéŦčį.


Submit Your Enquiries to Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO) HERE
Â 




Taught postgraduate programmes


Entry scholarships may be provided by individual programme offering academic departments. Please visit the page "Duration & Credit Requirement" under individual programmes listed onÂ Taught Postgraduate AdmissionsÂ webpage for scholarship availability and further enquiries.


Submit Your Enquiries to Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO) HERE
Â 




Research postgraduate programmes


Please visit Fellowship & Scholarship Schemes for postgraduate research students and contact the Graduate School for details and further enquiries.

Submit Your Enquiries to Graduate School (GS) HERE















Post-entry Scholarships - awarded on the basis of PolyU academic results




a) Post-entry Scholarships/ Prizes awarded on the basis of academic and/or non-academic achievements
From time to time, a number of private organisations, firms, professional bodies and individuals donate to PolyU scholarships and prizes which are awarded to students with outstanding academic and/or non-academic achievements (such as achievements in extra-curricular activities and community services) during their studies at PolyU.Â  Most of the scholarships/prizes are awarded to undergraduate students and the awardees are normally nominated by Heads of Academic Departments.Â  Please clickÂ hereÂ for the list of post-entry scholarships* available for undergraduate/taught postgraduate students.Â  However, a limited number of scholarships/prizes are open for application by students or nomination by academic departments via SAO.Â  Please refer toÂ theÂ scheduleÂ of inviting application/nomination for details.
*For information on entry scholarships for PolyU taught postgraduate/undergraduate students or entry/post-entry scholarships for research postgraduate students, please visit the webpage of Student Admissions and Scholarships Office (SASO)Â or Graduate School (GS) respectively for details. 
b) Scholarships for Student Exchange
PolyU offers scholarships/sponsorship/subsidy/financial assistance to support the living costs of full-time UGC-funded undergraduate students during exchange studies overseas/in Mainland China, mainly via the Global Engagement Office (GEO). Please visit the website ofÂ GEO websiteÂ [refer to the part "Financial Support"] for further details.
Some scholarships are also made available to full-time current students to pursue non-local studies.Â  Most of these scholarships are awarded to undergraduate students for attending exchange programmes, and the awardees are normally nominated by Heads of Academic Departments.Â  Please clickÂ hereÂ and contact the Department concerned for details. Some scholarships may specifically require the applicants/nominees to have the TOEFL or IELTS results for the Donor's consideration of the applications/nominations.Â  Students are recommended to attempt theÂ TOEFLÂ orÂ IELTSÂ examinationÂ (Academic Module) first if you are interested in these scholarships.
Besides, the University has set up theÂ Non-Local Study Fund (NLSF)Â to support students in pursuing non-local learning activities. Students who undertake aÂ Service-LearningÂ orÂ Cluster-Area Requirements (CAR)Â subject with non-local learning elements will be eligible for funding support up to HKD$10,000.

Special Announcement



15 Jul 2026

The PolyU Job Board will be temporarily suspended and unavailable on 21 July 2026 (Tuesday), from 8:00 AM to 8:00 PM due to system maintenance. Services will resume after maintenance is completed. Should you experience any issues afterwards, please feel free to contact the Careers and Placement Section, Student Affairs Office at career.cps@polyu.edu.hk for assistance.
